telltalePatterns: /w/ to [v] substitution in water and walk
dental fricative /θ/ to [s] substitution in think
final obstruent devoicing in bread and red

prosody.intonation: L1-influenced flat contours with abrupt terminal falls
prosody.fluencyNotes: Occasional lack of connected speech; heavy use of glottal stops before vowel-initial words

recommendedFocus.area: Labial-velar approximant /w/ and labiodental fricative /v/ distinction
recommendedFocus.reason: Prevents lexical confusion (e.g., wet vs vet) and strongly marks the L1 influence on intelligibility
Substituting with alveolar fricatives /s/ and /z/ noticeably impacts native-like quality

result.transcript: Yesterday, the weather was really beautiful, so I decided to go for a walk through the park near my house. The trees were green, and the birds were singing everywhere. I think spring is my favorite time of the year. I walked along the river and watched some children playing with a big red ball. They were laughing and having so much fun together. One little girl threw the ball very high, and it landed right in the water. Her father had to reach in and pull it out. After that, I stopped at a small village shop to buy some bread and a bottle of juice.
